compiled_state.title: dso:sprint: Skill tool invoked twice on session load -> orchestrator falls back to Read tool instead of consuming Skill result
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

After a successful Skill(dso:sprint) invocation, the orchestrator consumes the skill content returned by the Skill tool and executes the sprint workflow without re-invoking the tool or reading the SKILL.md file directly.

#### Actual Behavior

On session load, the orchestrator called Skill(dso:sprint) twice (both returned 'Successfully loaded skill'), then stated 'I have the SKILL.md content' and proceeded to execute Phase 1 — indicating it read the SKILL.md file via the Read tool instead of consuming the Skill tool result.

### 6. Logs

```
Skill(dso:sprint)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

Skill(dso:sprint)
  ⎿  Successfully loaded skill

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

I have the SKILL.md content. Let me now proceed with Phase 1 of the sprint.
```

comments.body: Same failure pattern observed with dso:debug-everything in session worktree-20260409-173013: Skill(dso:debug-everything) invoked twice (both returned 'Successfully loaded skill'), then the orchestrator read SKILL.md directly via Read tool and proceeded to execute the skill inline. The Skill tool result is not being consumed as the execution context in either case — the orchestrator treats the Skill invocation as a loading step and then reaches for the file directly.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
Investigation complete. Root cause: HOOK-INJECTION.md and SKILL.md do not explain what 'Successfully loaded skill' (the Skill tool return value) means. The model sees a confirmation without visible content in the tool result and interprets it as a loading step — then reaches for the file directly via Read.

Mechanism: When a Skill is invoked in Claude Code, the actual skill content is injected via a <system-reminder> block, NOT in the Skill tool's visible result text. 'Successfully loaded skill' means the content IS in context. Without guidance explaining this, the model assumes the content was not delivered and falls back to reading the SKILL.md file directly, bypassing skill isolation.

Fix: Added explicit guidance to both HOOK-INJECTION.md and SKILL.md:
  'When the Skill tool returns Successfully loaded skill: The skill content IS now available — it appears in a <system-reminder> block in your context. Do NOT invoke the Skill tool a second time. Do NOT use the Read tool to read the skill file.'

RED tests: tests/skills/test-using-lockpick-clarification.sh (test_hook_md_has_successfully_loaded_guidance + test_skill_md_has_successfully_loaded_guidance). GREEN after fix.
comments.author: Test
